reference.domains: (topic reference) (topic abbrev-d) a(props deliveryTarget) (topic equation-d) (topic hazard-d) (topic hi-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic markup-d) (topic mathml-d) (topic pr-d) (topic relmgmt-d) (topic sw-d) (topic svg-d) (topic ui-d) (topic ut-d) (topic markup-d xml-d)
reference.title: Quick reference

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Single-page quick reference for markdown-validator: CLI commands, operators, flags, workflow step tokens, and exit codes.

reference.shortdesc: All operators, flags, workflow step tokens, CLI options, and exit codes on one page. Use this page when you know what you need and just want the symbol or syntax.

section.title: CLI commands

row.entry: Validate a single file and print a report.
Validate all .md files in the directory recursively. Prints one report per file and a summary.

entry.codeblock: md-validate validate DIR/ --rules RULES \ --format json --output reports/

row.entry: Validate and save one report file per document to reports/. Formats: text, json, csv.
Enable DEBUG-level logging to trace rule and workflow evaluation step by step.
Suppress all output; use only the exit code (0 = pass, 1 = fail).
Start the interactive REPL for authoring and testing rules. See REPL commands reference.

section.p: Full option list: CLI options reference.

row.entry: All Required rules passed. Suggested failures do not change this code.
One or more Required rules failed, or a file has no parseable front matter. In a directory scan, one failing file sets the code for the entire run.

section.title: Flags
section.p: The flag field controls how the query result is processed before comparison.

row.entry: Raw string value of the front-matter key.
Returns true if the key exists, false if absent. Use == true by convention.
Parses the value as a date; computes age in days as (today − date).days. Use < to require freshness within N days.
Applies the operator as a regex match against the front-matter value.
Count of XPath matches. Returns an integer string.
Text content of the first XPath match. Empty string if no match.
Comma-separated list of HTML tag names of matched elements.
All text content of matched elements including nested text. Typically used with /html/body.

section.p: Full descriptions and examples: Flags reference.

section.title: Operators
section.p: The operation field specifies the comparison between the processed query result and value.

row.entry: Result contains value as a substring (case-insensitive); for comma-separated lists, at least one value must match.
Sentence count ≤ value. Requires NLTK corpora.
Nth word's POS tag equals value (Penn Treebank tags, 1-indexed). Requires NLTK corpora.

section.p: Full descriptions and examples: Operators reference.

section.title: Workflow step tokens
section.p: A workflow's steps string is a comma-separated list of source-target tokens. N represents a rule ID.

row.entry: Start: load rule N as the initial state.
Rule N's result ends the workflow.
Rule N's result becomes the decision (branch) point.
If the decision is True (pass), load rule N.
If the decision is False (fail), load rule N.
Rule N's result exits to the merge state.
Merge state exits to rule N (continues the workflow).
Merge state becomes the decision point.

dlentry.dt: Conditional (run B only when A passes)

section.p: Full step patterns with examples: Workflow step patterns reference.
